behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: source collectors -> aggregator -> summary-step input. A unit test of one parser would miss missing/overwritten fields during wiring. Real OS/network is not required for this behavior; in-process stubs for the three source clients can reproduce it. Rejected higher levels: System is disqualified by behavior text focusing on "content passed" (handoff correctness, not real network conditions). Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because this behavior does not assert model decision quality, only payload assembly before generation.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real collection/orchestration modules with stubbed source adapters returning distinct sentinel values; invoke one report build; assert the exact payload sent to the summary-generation boundary contains all three source sections/keys.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module resilience logic: source failure propagation + section-level fallback rendering + continued report assembly. A single-function unit test would miss orchestration behavior. Real infrastructure is not strictly required; a stubbed adapter throwing the same failure class can trigger the path. Rejected higher levels: System is disqualified by behavior text emphasizing fallback outcome ("report is produced...rather than aborting"), which can be validated via in-process failure injection. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because correctness here is fallback control flow, not model-output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use real orchestrator/report builder modules; configure one source adapter to fail and the other two to return data; assert run completes, failed section contains `data unavailable`, and remaining sections are present.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly about summary quality/faithfulness, which depends on real model output decisions (what to include, how to phrase, possible omissions/hallucinations). A deterministic stub would always pass and hide real failures. Rejected higher level: Workflow is disqualified by behavior text describing one generation judgment (single summary step), not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run summary generation against a fixed dataset with known key values using the real model/API; score factual coverage/consistency against required key facts (e.g., must include specified metric/issue/deploy values) across repeated runs with acceptance thresholds.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes real external interfaces (source APIs + email transport/inbox delivery). In-process mocks can hide protocol/auth/config/TLS/provider behavior and mailbox delivery issues. Rejected higher levels: Agentic is disqualified by behavior text focusing on end-to-end delivery ("reaches ... inbox correctly") rather than evaluating model-output quality; Workflow is disqualified because no multi-agentic decision chain is specified.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the scheduled script (or equivalent entrypoint) against real/staging source endpoints and a real test mailbox; verify a message is actually delivered to configured recipients with expected report structure/sections.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior couples real connectivity failure semantics with persistence and cross-run retry behavior. Mocking mail delivery can miss real unreachable-error modes from the transport stack; cross-run verification is stronger with real process/file behavior. Rejected higher levels: Agentic is disqualified because this is delivery-retry infrastructure, not model judgment. Workflow is disqualified because multi-step here is operational scheduling/retry, not multiple agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run 1: execute script with email endpoint made unreachable (e.g., blocked host/port) and verify fallback report file is written. Run 2 (next scheduled invocation simulation with real entrypoint): restore email service, execute again, verify previously saved report is retried/delivered and fallback state is cleared/updated appropriately.
